title: Days Of Wine And Dohses

jokes.text: Ah, trash night. In France they call it 'la nuit de poubelle.' In Germany it's 'crappenfest.'
jokes.setup: Homer explains foreign terms for trash night with scholarly authority
jokes.punchline: 'Crappenfest' - absurd fake German word combining 'crap' and 'fest'
jokes.explanation: Classic Simpsons fake foreign language joke with perfect German-sounding construction. Homer's fake intellectualism makes it character-specific.

jokes.text: I can't believe it only comes 52 times a year.
jokes.setup: Homer waxing poetic about trash night's rarity
jokes.punchline: Treating weekly occurrence as disappointingly infrequent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - treating mundane activity as special occasion. Character-driven absurdity.

jokes.text: And remember, the best stuff is usually deep in the garbage juice.
jokes.setup: Homer giving trash-diving advice
jokes.punchline: Disgusting detail about 'garbage juice' presented as wisdom
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor elevated by Homer's earnest delivery as helpful tip. Term 'garbage juice' is memorably disgusting.

jokes.text: Lookee here! Cardy-board tubes! Now we can have indoor plumbin', just like they's got at the women's lockup.
jokes.setup: Cletus excited about found cardboard tubes
jokes.punchline: His idea of luxury is comparing to women's prison plumbing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character writing - Cletus's twisted reference point reveals his background. Dark implication delivered innocently.

jokes.text: They spoilt you, Brandine. Sometimes I don't even know who you are anymore.
jokes.setup: Cletus responding to Brandine's prison comment
jokes.punchline: Acting like indoor plumbing has corrupted his wife's simple values
jokes.explanation: Perfect relationship dynamic - treating basic amenities as corrupting luxury. Cletus's wounded pride over 'spoiling' is absurd.

jokes.text: Look like Milhouse's mom finally threw out his blankie. He'll pay a lot to get this back. Especially when we send it to him piece by piece.
jokes.setup: Bullies find Milhouse's blankie in trash
jokes.punchline: Escalating from ransom to psychological torture plan
jokes.explanation: Dark escalation works well - from simple extortion to sadistic plan. Character-appropriate cruelty with comedic timing.

jokes.text: It seems I will never sell these She-Hulk vs. Leon Spinks comics. Worst crossover ever.
jokes.setup: Comic Book Guy lamenting unsellable comics
jokes.punchline: Absurd crossover between Marvel character and real boxer, delivered in his signature style
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy moment - absurd premise delivered with his trademark dismissive authority. 'Worst crossover ever' became iconic catchphrase format.

jokes.text: Well, this muscle shirt's a pretty good find. / Dad, that's a sports bra. / All I know is I'm finally getting the support I need.
jokes.setup: Homer proudly wearing found 'muscle shirt'
jokes.punchline: It's a sports bra, but Homer focuses only on the functional benefit
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness - missing the embarrassment, focusing on practical benefit. Good three-part structure with perfect character logic.

jokes.text: Hey, Vinnie. How's about a pizza? / I got-a no job.
jokes.setup: Homer talking to discarded barber hair
jokes.punchline: He voices both sides of conversation, giving hair Italian accent and unemployment
jokes.explanation: Absurdist Homer moment - creating full character for hair clippings. The unemployment detail adds unexpected pathos to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Get out of my dreams and into my car.
jokes.setup: Homer finding something appealing in Hawaiian restaurant trash
jokes.punchline: Quoting Billy Ocean song lyrics in this context
jokes.explanation: Mild pop culture reference humor. Homer's random song quoting is in character but not particularly clever here.

jokes.text: I don't remember the air in the kitchen being so wavy.
jokes.setup: Visual effect of gas leak making air wavy
jokes.punchline: Homer's oblivious description of dangerous gas leak as aesthetic observation
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness - treating deadly gas leak as mild curiosity about air texture. Sets up the tiki god sequence.

jokes.text: Behold! I am King Talkie Tiki! / Hey, Flanders. Can your god do that?
jokes.setup: Homer using tiki head and gas leak to create fire effects
jokes.punchline: Immediately challenging Flanders' religion with his 'miracle'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - immediately using cheap trick to challenge neighbor's faith. Visual gag combined with character-driven dialogue.

jokes.text: A-Actually, Homer, you and I worship the same god, so- / Irregardless! I am your god now.
jokes.setup: Ned trying to reasonably explain shared Christian faith
jokes.punchline: Homer dismisses with 'irregardless' (non-word) and declares himself god
jokes.explanation: 'Irregardless' is perfect Homer malapropism - confident use of non-word to dismiss logic. Classic dynamic between reasonable Ned and absurd Homer.

jokes.text: I am all power- [Screams] I'll be at Moe's.
jokes.setup: Homer declaring omnipotence as tiki god
jokes.punchline: Immediately injured by his own gas leak, deflated to mundane bar visit
jokes.explanation: Perfect puncturing of Homer's grandiose moment. Quick transition from god-complex to mundane escape to bar is excellent timing.

jokes.text: So I says to the cop, 'No... you're driving under the influence of bein' a jerk!'
jokes.setup: Barney telling story about encounter with police
jokes.punchline: Turning DUI accusation back on cop with childish logic
jokes.explanation: Decent Barney drunk logic - childish comeback that probably made situation worse. In character but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Hey, Barney, what's with the glum face? You glum or somethin'? Huh, glummy?
jokes.setup: Homer noticing Barney's mood
jokes.punchline: Repetitive, childish variations on 'glum' showing Homer's limited vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Mild Homer speech pattern humor - his limited vocabulary leading to repetitive word play. Competent but unremarkable.

jokes.text: You know, it was my birthday last week and no one remembered. / What are you, nuts? / I threw you a party at my house.
jokes.setup: Barney complaining no one remembered his birthday
jokes.punchline: Homer and Moe both immediately contradict him - he was too drunk to remember his own party
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Barney's alcoholism reality check. The immediate dual contradiction works well, sets up video evidence.

jokes.text: Oh, that's it, baby. All for Moe. Oh, yeah, work the slot. Show me the package. Whoa. That's-That's a project I'm workin' on.
jokes.setup: Suggestive audio that sounds like pornography
jokes.punchline: It's actually Moe talking to slot machine, then embarrassed explanation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection gag - sexual language revealed as gambling addiction. Moe's pathetic embarrassment adds character depth.

jokes.text: I'm just sayin' that when we die... there's gonna be a planet for the French, a planet for the Chinese... and we'll all be a lot happier.
jokes.setup: Barney philosophizing while drunk
jokes.punchline: Bizarre afterlife theory involving racial segregation presented as positive
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor - drunk Barney's warped utopian vision. Uncomfortable racial implications delivered with innocent intent.

jokes.text: Gee. Is that what I look like when I'm drunk? / You wish. That's the stage we call 'Professor Barney.'
jokes.setup: Barney asking if video shows him drunk
jokes.punchline: Moe reveals this is actually his coherent stage, nicknamed 'Professor Barney'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation setup - what Barney thinks is his worst is actually his best. 'Professor Barney' is a perfect nickname.

jokes.text: Well, I'm off to market. [High-pitched voice and burp]
jokes.setup: Showing actual drunk Barney behavior
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous high voice combined with gross burp, mundane announcement
jokes.explanation: Delivers on promise of showing drunk Barney. Physical comedy with voice change and bodily function, but fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Marge, you're making a complete fool of yours- Oh, it's just Barney.
jokes.setup: Homer criticizing someone's foolish behavior
jokes.punchline: Realizes it's Barney and immediately dismisses concern
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Homer's standards instantly drop when he realizes it's Barney. Shows their relationship dynamic efficiently.

jokes.text: Precious alcohol, soaking in the shag! [gulping and growling sounds]
jokes.setup: Barney seeing spilled alcohol in carpet
jokes.punchline: Desperately trying to consume alcohol from carpet fibers
jokes.explanation: Dark physical comedy showing depths of Barney's addiction. 'Precious alcohol' language adds Gollum-like obsession element.

jokes.text: Oh. How embarrassing. / Well, how did this happen? / Oh, that. You've had that for a while.
jokes.setup: Barney noticing embarrassing stain or mark
jokes.punchline: Homer and Moe casually reveal it's been there so long they forgot about it
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Barney's deterioration. Friends' casual acceptance of his decline adds pathos to humor.

jokes.text: I still got mine. Barney, I got you what no drunk can do without- morning-after stationery.
jokes.setup: Homer giving Barney a belated birthday present
jokes.punchline: Gift specifically designed for drunk person's apology needs
jokes.explanation: Clever gift idea that acknowledges Barney's pattern of drunken behavior requiring apologies. Dark but practical humor.

jokes.text: Can you imagine this booze-bag at the wheel of a whirlybird? He'd be all, 'Look at me! I'm a tanked-up loser in a helicopter!'
jokes.setup: Homer mocking the idea of Barney flying a helicopter
jokes.punchline: Cruelly imitating what drunk Barney would say, calling him names
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's casual cruelty toward his 'friend.' The mock quote is mean-spirited but sets up Barney's motivation to change.

jokes.text: So, I'm a tanked-up loser? Is that how you see me? / Oh, sounds like a certain loser could use some tankin' up.
jokes.setup: Barney hurt by Homer's cruel description
jokes.punchline: Homer doubles down, suggesting Barney needs more alcohol
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Homer's insensitivity triggers Barney's crisis. Homer's solution (more drinking) shows his cluelessness.

jokes.text: Wait a minute, Barney. You gotta be sober to fly. I mean, it's not like driving a car.
jokes.setup: Homer warning Barney about needing sobriety to fly
jokes.punchline: Implies drunk driving is acceptable, just not drunk flying
jokes.explanation: Dark Homer logic - distinguishing between drunk driving (apparently fine) and drunk flying (dangerous). Perfect character blindspot.

jokes.text: Wait. That ain't funny. He's my best customer. Well, the handwriting's on the wall. To stay afloat, this bar is gonna have to go queer.
jokes.setup: Moe realizing losing Barney hurts business
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to targeting gay clientele as business strategy
jokes.explanation: Shows Moe's mercenary business thinking. The casual way he considers changing his bar's identity is character-appropriate cynicism.

jokes.text: You mean it's not? Oh. Wrong again, Gay Guide to Springfield.
jokes.setup: Someone correcting Moe's assumption about his bar
jokes.punchline: Moe consulting and dismissing a gay guidebook he apparently owns
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up gag. That Moe owns and consults gay guidebooks adds unexpected character detail and humor.

jokes.text: All right, world. Get ready to meet the clean and sober Barney Gumble. [immediately] Whee! Give me a beer.
jokes.setup: Barney making grand declaration of sobriety
jokes.punchline: Immediately orders beer upon entering bar
jokes.explanation: Classic addiction comedy - immediate contradiction of sobriety vow. Perfect timing and character consistency with his weakness.

jokes.text: Santeria, you're the greatest.
jokes.setup: Barney expressing gratitude to bartender
jokes.punchline: Calling Moe 'Santeria' (voodoo religion) instead of his name
jokes.explanation: Good drunk malapropism - confusing Moe's name with mystical practice. Shows Barney's altered mental state amusingly.

jokes.text: I feel for you, pally, but, uh, you want A.A. This is Triple 'A.'
jokes.setup: Barney saying 'My name is Barney, and I'm an alcoholic'
jokes.punchline: He's at Auto Club (AAA), not Alcoholics Anonymous (AA)
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection gag. Perfect setup with AA confession format, brilliant payoff with acronym confusion. Very quotable and well-constructed.

jokes.text: My name is Homer, and I'm planning a trip to Saint Louis. / East Saint Louis? / Is there any other Saint Louis?
jokes.setup: Homer adapting to AAA format with travel plans
jokes.punchline: Employee asks about dangerous East St. Louis, Homer's clueless response
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to AAA gag. Homer's geographic ignorance and employee's concern about dangerous area work well together.

jokes.text: I see you finally hit rock bottom. / Not a chance. I can sink way lower.
jokes.setup: AA leader assuming Homer has hit rock bottom
jokes.punchline: Homer proudly boasts he can deteriorate further
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - taking pride in his potential for further degradation. Subverts expected AA humility with characteristic ego.

jokes.text: And that's when you realized you were an alcoholic? / Oh, no, I never touch the stuff. But you don't have to be drunk to know the value of Amway.
jokes.setup: Gil sharing story that seems about alcoholism
jokes.punchline: Reveals he's not an alcoholic, just trying to sell Amway products
jokes.explanation: Excellent Gil moment - using AA meeting as sales opportunity. Perfect character integration with his desperate salesman persona.

jokes.text: Anything that takes 12 steps isn't worth doin'. Get it? Huh? Twelve? Steps?
jokes.setup: Homer complaining about walking to cookie table
jokes.punchline: Accidentally makes pun on AA's 12-step program, then over-explains it
jokes.explanation: Decent accidental pun that Homer ruins by over-explaining. The laziness combined with the coincidental wordplay is very Homer.

jokes.text: Sorry, pal, but it takes a special kind of man to pilot these birds. / I got a coupon. / Okay, get in.
jokes.setup: Instructor claiming high standards for pilot candidates
jokes.punchline: Immediately abandons standards when coupon is presented
jokes.explanation: Excellent corporate cynicism gag - safety standards immediately abandoned for money. Perfect economy and structure.

jokes.text: You know who else I like is that, uh, Leo Sayer.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation during helicopter lesson
jokes.punchline: Random reference to obscure 70s singer Leo Sayer
jokes.explanation: Mild character humor - middle-aged instructor's dated musical references. Realistic but not particularly strong comedy.

jokes.text: One sec. Testing, black box. One, two. [Screaming]
jokes.setup: Instructor testing emergency recording device
jokes.punchline: Immediately followed by screaming as helicopter goes out of control
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy timing - professional black box test immediately followed by disaster. Implies this happens often enough to warrant testing.

jokes.text: It's okay. That's what the diapers are for.
jokes.setup: Barney apologizing for poor flying
jokes.punchline: Instructor casually mentions wearing diapers for student flights
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - reveals instructor's practical preparation for terror. Matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Is that Rainier Wolfcastle? / Check out the gut. / It's for a movie. I'm playing a fat secret agent.
jokes.setup: Kids noticing actor's weight gain
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's for a role - fat secret agent movie
jokes.explanation: Mild celebrity satire. The absurd movie premise (fat secret agent) is amusing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Are you trying to make me the new Barney? / Hey, every bar needs a world-class drunk.
jokes.setup: Homer suspicious about being given Barney's seat
jokes.punchline: Friends confirm they need someone to fill the 'drunk' role
jokes.explanation: Good group dynamic humor - the casual way they discuss needing a designated drunk shows their dysfunction.

jokes.text: Now dance, rummy. / O-Okay. [humming and dancing]
jokes.setup: Friends commanding Homer to dance
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately complies despite being demeaned
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable power dynamic humor. Homer's eager compliance despite humiliation shows his desperate need for acceptance.

jokes.text: You danced for hours just because they told you to? / If I didn't, I'd lose their respect.
jokes.setup: Barney questioning Homer's dignity
jokes.punchline: Homer thinks degrading performance earns respect
jokes.explanation: Good character psychology - Homer's backwards logic about earning respect through humiliation. Shows his social insecurity.

jokes.text: He hangs newspapers over the urinals now. You can read the sports page while you pee. Very la-di-da.
jokes.setup: Homer describing bar improvements to Barney
jokes.punchline: Treating basic convenience as excessive luxury
jokes.explanation: Good Homer class consciousness - viewing minor convenience as pretentious luxury. 'La-di-da' is perfect dismissive phrase.

jokes.text: Remember the day we jumped that census guy and stole his clicker? Those are all priceless memories, Homer...
jokes.setup: Barney reminiscing about good times with Homer
jokes.punchline: Their 'fond memory' involves assault and theft of government worker
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark comedy - treating violent crime as cherished friendship memory. The specificity of 'census guy' and 'clicker' makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Cheer up, Homie. You don't need friends to be happy. I haven't had a friend in years. / But you got me. Who have I got?
jokes.setup: Marge trying to console Homer about friendship
jokes.punchline: Homer doesn't count his wife as a friend, revealing marital dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Dark marital humor - Homer's casual dismissal of his wife as friend is both funny and sad. Good relationship dynamics.

jokes.text: Oh, Lenny and Carl suck! Please don't tell Lenny and Carl I said that, 'cause if I ever lost 'em as friends-
jokes.setup: Homer claiming he has other friends
jokes.punchline: Immediately badmouths them but desperately needs them
jokes.explanation: Good Homer contradiction - insulting friends while panicking about losing them. Shows his social desperation and lack of self-awareness.

jokes.text: Old friends stick together, like O.J. and A.C., or the Falcon and the Snowman.
jokes.setup: Marge giving advice about friendship loyalty
jokes.punchline: Uses examples of criminal partnerships - O.J. Simpson's friend and spy movie
jokes.explanation: Dark irony - Marge unknowingly cites criminal loyalty as positive examples. The cultural references work for dark comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, why can't I have a nickname?
jokes.setup: Homer hearing about criminal partnerships
jokes.punchline: His takeaway is jealousy over not having a nickname
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer focus - missing the criminal implications, fixating on superficial detail. Very character-appropriate response.

jokes.text: Hey, Apu, you got any of those potato chips that give you diarrhea? I need to do a little spring cleaning.
jokes.setup: Homer asking Apu for specific product
jokes.punchline: Wants laxative chips for intentional digestive cleansing
jokes.explanation: Gross-out humor with Homer's casual approach to digestive issues. 'Spring cleaning' euphemism makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Beer, huh? / That's right. / Enjoy. / That I will. / Then we agree. / You keep thinking that.
jokes.setup: Former friends having stilted conversation
jokes.punchline: Increasingly meaningless exchange showing their relationship breakdown
jokes.explanation: Good awkward dialogue showing friendship strain. The escalating passive-aggression in mundane exchange is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Crescent moon! Crescent moon!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to get good photo for contest
jokes.punchline: Shouting at moon to change shape for better picture
jokes.explanation: Mild kid logic humor - Bart trying to command celestial bodies. Absurd but not particularly memorable.

jokes.text: To me, you're true-blue. / Aw, thanks, big guy. / Now dance, rummy. / Okay.
jokes.setup: Homer giving heartfelt tribute to friends
jokes.punchline: They immediately demean him again, he complies
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier humiliation. The immediate shift from sentiment to degradation shows the toxic friendship dynamic.

jokes.text: Moe, I've come here to make amends for my disgraceful behavior over the last 20 years. / Oh, that's okay, Barn. / No, it's not okay.
jokes.setup: Barney making formal AA-style amends
jokes.punchline: Moe tries to dismiss it, Barney insists on confessing
jokes.explanation: Good recovery program humor. Sets up for escalating confession of disgusting behavior. Barney's earnestness works well.

jokes.text: I broke barstools, befouled your broom closet... and made sweet love to your pool table, which I then befouled.
jokes.setup: Barney listing his shameful behaviors
jokes.punchline: Escalating from property damage to bestiality to more property damage
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and word choice. 'Made sweet love' euphemism for furniture, followed by 'befouled' creates perfect rhythm. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Well, I play Jerry 'Fireball' Mudflap... a feisty Supreme Court justice, who's searching for his birth mother... while competing in a cross-country fire truck race. It's garbage.
jokes.setup: Burt Reynolds describing his new movie
jokes.punchline: Absurdly complex plot combining unrelated elements, then honest assessment
jokes.explanation: Excellent Hollywood satire. The ridiculous high-concept plot description followed by blunt 'It's garbage' is perfect celebrity honesty.

jokes.text: Barney, the call is from heroism. Will you accept the charges?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to motivate Barney for rescue mission
jokes.punchline: Frames heroism as collect phone call requiring acceptance
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay turning dramatic moment into phone operator metaphor

jokes.text: You can't drink 'em all. / Oh, yes, I can. / I'll- [Homer drinks them] Ew, it's warm.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to prevent Barney from drinking
jokes.punchline: Homer drinks the beers himself, complains about temperature
jokes.explanation: Good Homer logic - saves friend by drinking the alcohol himself, still complains about quality. Perfect selfish heroism.

jokes.text: I love you. Let's not lose touch after graduation. [snoring]
jokes.setup: Homer drunk and emotional from helping Barney
jokes.punchline: Treats current situation like high school graduation farewell
jokes.explanation: Good drunk Homer sentimentality. The inappropriate graduation reference shows his confused emotional state.

jokes.text: You brave man. You took six silver bullets for me. Stay away from my wife.
jokes.setup: Barney praising Homer's sacrifice
jokes.punchline: Calls beers 'silver bullets,' then random jealous warning
jokes.explanation: Decent absurd dialogue. The sudden shift from gratitude to possessiveness is random but fits Barney's confused state.

jokes.text: You did it, Dad! / You can't prove I did it. / No! You saved our lives.
jokes.setup: Kids celebrating Homer's heroic rescue
jokes.punchline: Homer assumes he's being accused of wrongdoing, defensive response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer psychology - can't process praise, assumes guilt. The defensive response to celebration is excellent character work.

jokes.text: You did it, Dad! / You can't prove I did it. / No! You saved our lives. / I could do a lot of things if I had some money.
jokes.setup: Kids praising Homer for saving them
jokes.punchline: Homer's defensive response then non-sequitur about needing money
jokes.explanation: Homer's instinctive defensiveness followed by random tangent about money - classic character flaw

jokes.text: Isn't it adorable? I guess some baby pictures were on that old roll of film. / Oh, we'll be the laughingstock of the whole town.
jokes.setup: Marge delighted by phone book cover photo
jokes.punchline: Reveals embarrassing baby pictures instead of intended photo
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to earlier setup. The contrast between Marge's delight and kids' horror works well.

jokes.text: Actually, I took the picture, so I gave my prize to the orphanage. / What? / How could you? / Just kidding. I would never do that.
jokes.setup: Marge claims she donated prize to charity
jokes.punchline: Kids outraged, she reveals it was a joke about her own selfishness
jokes.explanation: Good Marge moment - rare selfishness joke. Her honest admission that she'd never be charitable is refreshingly cynical.

jokes.text: So what do you call this stuff? / A double tall mocha latte. / It's not bad. Well, it ain't beer.
jokes.setup: Homer and Barney bonding over coffee instead of alcohol
jokes.punchline: Grudging acceptance of coffee, still comparing to beer
jokes.explanation: Mild friendship resolution humor. Shows their relationship adapting to Barney's sobriety, though Homer still misses beer.

jokes.text: But at least I got that monkey off my back. [gulping sounds] Oh. [more gulping] Oh. / Ha, ha. Nobody gets away from Moe. Nobody.
jokes.setup: Barney celebrating his sobriety victory
jokes.punchline: Reveals coffee is addictive too, Moe's sinister satisfaction
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark ending. Barney trades one addiction for another, Moe's villainous glee at maintaining customers. Perfect cynical conclusion.

tmdb_overview: After being embarrassed by a drunken video of himself, Barney vows to sober up. Newly clean, he gets work as a helicopter pilot.
